title: Authentication, Passwords, Sessions, Cookies, and JOSE · Type-System Trace: Jwks Rotation Cache
summary: The server stores weak password material, keeps session identifiers across privilege changes, decodes JWTs without validating them, accepts arbitrary algorithms, and treats possession as authorization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts jwks rotation cache increment in credential verification and principal-construction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound JWKS retrieval, issuer mapping, cache lifetime, refresh, key rotation, key identifiers, algorithm confusion, stale behavior, and outage response.
Use jose verification with explicit algorithms, trusted keys, issuer, audience, expiration, not-before, clock tolerance, token type, and bounded claim validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Jwks Rotation Cache · Theory Jwks Rotation Cache
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate bounded token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe.
